2606.22837.title: CLIP-guided Diffusion Model for Backdoor Generation in Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition
2606.22837.paper_summary: Sensors are critical components of modern intelligent devices. The proliferation of the Internet of Things (IoT) and wearable mobile devices has enabled the integration of such sensors to monitor the environment and enable users to take predictive actions. Human activity recognition (HAR) is a popular application in which Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU)-based sensors, such as accelerometers and gyroscopes, are used to provide insights into health, training, and medical diagnosis. However, the accuracy of such a model is hindered by the lack of data. The diffusion model-based technique has proven successful in generating synthetic data for training HAR models. In this paper, we propose a backdoor training technique, IMU-DM-CLIP, that leverages a diffusion model to enable trigger-based attacks on HAR models. Our empirical analysis shows that the attack is successful even with a very small backdoor injection rate of 10\% and 10\% of the data guided for the diffusion model.
2606.22837.paper_summary_zh: 感測器是現代智能設備的關鍵組件。物聯網 (IoT) 和可穿戴移動設備的普及使得這些感測器能夠整合以監測環境並使用戶能夠採取預測性行動。人類活動識別 (HAR) 是一個流行的應用，其中基於慣性測量單元 (IMU) 的感測器，如加速度計和陀螺儀，被用來提供健康、訓練和醫療診斷的見解。然而，這種模型的準確性受到數據不足的阻礙。基於擴散模型的技術已被證明在生成合成數據以訓練 HAR 模型方面成功。在本文中，我們提出了一種後門訓練技術 IMU-DM-CLIP，利用擴散模型來實現對 HAR 模型的觸發基礎攻擊。我們的實證分析顯示，即使在非常小的後門注入率 10\% 和 10\% 的數據指導擴散模型的情況下，攻擊仍然成功。
2606.22837.author: Toby Briston et.al.
2606.22837.authors: Toby Briston, Illya Kosyk, Kuniyih S
